subject: Re: Possible to use denoised tomograms for template matching?
body:
Yeah, I suppose thats how i would do it i.e. putting the denoised versions
in the reconstruction folder (before backing it up ofc)
Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK


On Wed, Jun 30, 2021 at 1:38 PM Matthias Vorländer <mvorlaender@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Hi Pranav,
>
> Thanks! Well, I just followed the instructions on the warp website. I am
> using 10000 iterations. Maybe its related to how much signal you still get
> in the reconstructions from only odd/even tilts? I am lucky to have huge
> particles in very thin ice, which might help.
>
> To make it work, would you just replace the tomos in the reconstruciton
> folder with the denoised ones?
>
> Thanks,
> Matthias
>
> p.sha...@gmail.com schrieb am Mittwoch, 30. Juni 2021 um 14:17:35 UTC+2:
>
>> Woow! That looks really good! Give it a go and tell us if it worked!
>> Also, how did you make Noise2Map work for you? I have had practically 0
>> success on a couple of datasets that I have!
>> Best,
>> Pranav
>> --
>> Pranav Shah
>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>>
>> Division of Structural Biology,
>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
>> University of Oxford,
>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
>> UK
>>
>>
>> On Wed, Jun 30, 2021 at 12:32 PM Matthias Vorländer <mvorl...@gmail.com>
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi there,
>>>
>>> I am wondering if it is possible to use  the denoised tomograms for
>>> template matching in warp? I just think the difference is so spectacular
>>> that it seems a shame not to make use of it (see attached images).
>>>
>>> Thanks a lot,
>>> Matthias
>>> [image: reconstructed.png][image: denoised.png][image: denoised.png]
>>>
